purpose: replace the teacher MoE function, not reconstruct teacher expert weights

bound_at_inference: layer identity, stored in the payload
forbidden_at_inference: any statistic of the evaluation target

output: the shared-plus-routed MoE output replacement, shape [.., 6144], which the block adds to the post-attention residual

physical_components.readout_weights: [hidden, 6144] float16, or [hidden, rank] and [rank, 6144] when factored
physical_components.bias: none; the fit is unbiased and the affine control measured the difference at 0.001 skill
physical_components.normalization: none inside the organ; the block's own norms are protected tensors
physical_components.selectors_or_gates: none; routing is removed, not approximated
physical_components.alignment: float16 payload is 2-byte aligned by construction
physical_components.per_layer_metadata: carried in the .gravity tensor descriptor, not in the payload

runtime_state.initialization_time: zero for the procedural grammar; the explicit grammar pays one 12.6 MB generation and upload per layer, measured at 1.58 ms per layer for the readout alone
runtime_state.note: a tiny seed with a large expanded runtime matrix is not zero cost, which is why both grammars are billed and why the procedural one won

determinism.reimplementation_gate: reproduce projection(width=8, hidden=4, seed=17) exactly; see GLM52_FUNCTIONAL_CPU_PARITY.json golden_vector
determinism.why_not_numpy_pcg64: the pilot fitted against NumPy's PCG64 with the Ziggurat normal, which is sequential and cannot be evaluated element-wise inside a Metal kernel. The frozen generator is a stateless hash so any element is addressable independently, which is what makes procedural in-kernel generation possible at all. Refitting under it changed layer-38 skill from 0.5318 to 0.5287, inside the four-seed spread of 0.0025.

honest_scope: one stage of one layer at a time, fitted on 12288 positions and scored on 4096 disjoint ones. Passing this contract is not capability and not a model.
